observations.recommendation:
1.5 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Budget Officer maintain the four (4) registries as required in Section 4(h)
of the Manual on NGAS to monitor appropriations, allotments received
and obligations incurred against said allotment and in order to prevent
incurrence of obligations in excess of allotments received or approved
appropriations. Said registries shall also be the basis in preparing the
Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts.

2.4 We have reiterated our previous recommendation that all financial
accounts and reports shall be submitted to the Commission on Audit every
10th day of the following month, and the year-end Financial Statements
and schedules be submitted on or before February 14 of each year for the
conduct of timely audit so as to facilitate timely communication of noted
deficiencies.

3.5 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer reconcile their accounts at least quarterly pursuant to COA
Circular No. 97-002 to immediately detect errors and prove the
correctness and accuracy of their records as well as to establish the
accuracy of cash balances. The reconciling items noted be taken up
immediately by both the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer in order to reflect the correct balance of the Cash in Bank,
Local Currency, Current account.

4.3 We have recommended that valid justifications be submitted to explain
the project deficiency. Further, corrective measures be undertaken to
correct the deficiency noted.

5.6 We have recommended the following:
5.6.1 The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to install
effective internal control on the municipality's collection system
including controls for the issuances, reporting, monitoring and
safeguarding of all accountable forms of the municipality;
5.6.2 The Municipal Treasurer to properly verify the Reports of
Collections and Deposits of all collectors and monitor issuances
and balances of accountable forms. Check the Statement of
Accountable Forms as to initial balances on hand, receipts, issues
and the ending balances on hand and make a physical count of
accountable forms remaining in the custody of the collectors and
check against ending balances on hand column;
5.6.3 The Municipal Treasurer to issue new set of accountable forms to
the collectors only when the previously issued accountable forms
are fully issued and properly reported in the RCDs;
5.6.4 The Municipal Treasurer to present all the unaccounted
accountable forms and submit the same to the Office of the
Auditor for verification and to come up with the audited Cash -
Local Treasury balance.
5.6.5 The Municipal Treasurer to submit Abstract of Collections in
order to establish the amount that should be reported, particularly
on the unreadable official receipts, both in the books of accounts
and cashbooks.
5.6.6 Valid justifications be submitted to explain the deficiencies noted
on the unaccounted/missing accountable forms, and the delayed
reporting of collections/late remittances.

6.11 We have recommended that:
6.11.1 The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit written justifications
particularly on the above-noted conflicting dates in the bidding and
procurement process as well as the other deficiencies noted for non-
compliance with the provisions of R.A. No. 9184.
6.11.2 Henceforth, the BAC ensure that the procuring entity strictly follow the
standards set forth by the procurement law and its Revised IRR, such
as on the posting of the required bid security before the bidders are
marked "passed", and the posting of performance security before the
awarding of contract and contract signing.

7.12 We have recommended that the BAC see to it that future winning bidders
comply with all the requirements set forth under the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A.
No. 9184 such as the enclosing of the required bid security, and the posting of
required performance security before the awarding of contract and contract
signing.

8.5 We have recommended that all procurements of the municipality should be
conducted through public bidding as mandated under R.A. No. 9184 and its
IRR, unless justified by the conditions provided under Rule XVI of the IRR as
recommended by the BAC and duly approved by the head of the procuring
entity. Alternative methods of procurement shall be resorted to only in highly
exceptional cases provided for in R.A. No. 9184. In all instances, the procuring
entity shall ensure that the most advantageous price for the LGU is obtained.

9.9 We have recommended that the personnel in-charge with release of contracts
to furnish the auditor with copies of these documents together with the
required attachments/supporting papers within five (5) days after perfection
thereof and ensure that the same are in the standard form as prescribed by
GPPB and as required by COA Circular No. 2009-001 for auditorial and
technical review. Likewise, submit immediately the still un-submitted CYs
2016 and 2017 contracts as enumerated above.

10.5 We have recommended that management submit written explanations on the
noted deficiencies and enforce the collection of the liquidated damages to the
contractors. The MPDC/TWG and the MDRRMO/Acting Municipal Engineer
determine the percentage of completion as of the target completion date for
the said projects and compute for the amount of the liquidated damages.
Henceforth, the LGU should properly monitor all its projects and in the event
that the contractor fail to accomplish the project within the expected delivery
period/contract duration, the related liquidated damages and other
appropriate actions be enforced immediately.

11.4 We have recommended that Management see to it that the budgeted amounts in
the SCBAA match with the approved budget. Henceforth, strictly utilize the fund
in accordance with the plan and budget.

12.7 We have recommended that management, in cases of lump sum
appropriations containing general terms, seek a separate SB appropriation
ordinance/resolution to serve as an authority to enter into contract in behalf of
the Municipal Government in accordance with Section 22 (c) of R.A. No. 7160
and the applicable jurisprudence.

13.8 We have recommended that the municipal mayor, before entering into
contract in behalf of the Municipal Government, ensure the existence of an
appropriation in accordance with Section 22 (c) of R.A. No. 7160 and the
afore-cited jurisprudence. See to it that fund availability is, as it has always
been, be an indispensable prerequisite to the execution of any government
contract involving the expenditure of public funds by all government agencies
at all levels.

14.4 We have recommended that Management carefully prepare the 20%
Development Fund Investment Plan. Utilize the fund appropriated for every
programs, projects and activities (PPAs) strictly in accordance with the plan and
budget to avoid over and under expenditure and partial/non-implementation of
PPAs. Lastly, limit expenditures to the appropriated amount for every PPA.

15.6 We have recommended that Management justify the above-noted lapses.
Ensure that utilization of the 20% Development Fund be limited on programs,
projects and activities that will contribute to the attainment of socio-economic
development and environmental management outcomes and shall partake the
nature of investment or capital expenditures that yield greater benefit to the
people in accordance with DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-
01.

16.4 We have recommended that management immediately submit the above-
enumerated documents stated in Annex E.5 for proper review and evaluation.

17.4 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget
Officer and the Municipal Mayor ensure that Cash Donation Utilization
Request (CDUR) are attached to the DV before the utilization of cash donation
for DRRM for proper monitoring and control. Also, prepare and maintain
separate subsidiary ledger (SL) for "Trust Liabilities-DRRMF" for cash
donations received amounting to ₱100,000.00 and above per donor per
purpose while those below ₱100,000.00 shall be grouped and posted in one SL
labeled as "Various Donors" per purpose.

18.6 We have recommended that management see to it that in cases of lump sum
appropriations containing general terms, a separate SB appropriation
ordinance/ resolution be secured by the Municipal Mayor to serve as an
authority to enter into contract in behalf of the Municipal Government in
accordance to Section 22(c) of R.A. No. 7160 and the applicable jurisprudence.

19.5 We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. Henceforth,
ensure the existence of an appropriation in accordance with Section 7 of R.A.
No. 9184 and Section 85(1) of P.D. No. 1445 and the afore-cited jurisprudence,
before transactions are made or contracts are entered into by the municipality.
Fund availability is, as it has always been, an indispensable prerequisite to the
execution of any government contract involving the expenditure of public
funds by all government agencies at all levels.

20.4 We have recommended that management:
20.4.1 strictly comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 10121 and the related
subsequent provisions on LDRRMF utilization.
20.4.2 ensure that personal services, maintenance and other operating
expenditures, and capital outlay of the LDRRMO as well as the regular
MOOE and capital outlay of the municipality are to be sourced from the
General Fund.
20.4.3 strictly utilize the LDRRMF for the implementation of DRRM
programs.
20.4.4 allot appropriations for MOOE, CO, and PS (for the required staffing)
under the LDRRMO and,
20.4.5 segregate clearly regular office expenses and to limit charges from the

We have recommended the following:

effective internal control on the municipality’s collection system
including controls for the issuances, reporting, monitoring and
safeguarding of all accountable forms of the municipality;

Collections and Deposits of all collectors and monitor issuances and

accountable forms remaining in the custody of the collectors and

balance.

establish the amount that should be reported, particularly on the
unreadable official receipts, both in the books of accounts and cashbooks.

unaccounted/missing accountable forms, and the delayed reporting of
collections/late remittances.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit
written justifications particularly on the above-noted conflicting dates in the
bidding and procurement process as well as the other deficiencies noted for
non-compliance with the provisions of R.A. No. 9184.
Henceforth, the BAC ensure that the procuring entity strictly follow the
standards set forth by the procurement law and its Revised IRR, such as on
the posting of the required bid security before the bidders are marked
“passed”, and the posting of performance security before the awarding of
contract and contract signing.

We have recommended that the BAC see to it that future winning bidders
comply with all the requirements set forth under the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A.
No. 9184 such as the enclosing of the required bid security, and the posting of
required performance security before the awarding of contract and contract
signing.

We have recommended that all procurements of the municipality should be
conducted through public bidding as mandated under R.A. No. 9184 and its
IRR, unless justified by the conditions provided under Rule XVI of the IRR as
recommended by the BAC and duly approved by the head of the procuring
entity. Alternative methods of procurement shall be resorted to only in highly
exceptional cases provided for in R.A. No. 9184. In all instances, the procuring
entity shall ensure that the most advantageous price for the LGU is obtained.

We have recommended that Management see to it that the budgeted amounts in
the SCBAA match with the approved budget. Henceforth, strictly utilize the
fund in accordance with the plan and budget.

We have recommended that management, in cases of lump sum
appropriations containing general terms, seek a separate SB appropriation
ordinance/resolution to serve as an authority to enter into contract in behalf of
the Municipal Government in accordance with Section 22 (c) of R.A. No. 7160
and the applicable jurisprudence.

We have recommended that the municipal mayor, before entering into
contract in behalf of the Municipal Government, ensure the existence of an
appropriation in accordance with Section 22 (c) of R.A. No. 7160 and the
applicable jurisprudence.
See to it that fund availability is, as it has always been, be an indispensable
prerequisite to the execution of any government contract involving the
expenditure of public funds by all government agencies at all levels.

We have recommended that Management carefully prepare the 20%
Development Fund Investment Plan. Utilize the fund appropriated for every
programs, projects and activities (PPAs) strictly in accordance with the plan and
budget to avoid over and under expenditure and partial/non-implementation of
PPAs. Lastly, limit expenditures to the appropriated amount for every PPA.

We have recommended that Management justify the above-noted lapses.
Ensure that utilization of the 20% Development Fund be limited on programs,
projects and activities that will contribute to the attainment of socio-economic
development and environmental management outcomes and shall partake the
nature of investment or capital expenditures that yield greater benefit to the
people in accordance with DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-
01.

observations.recommendation:
We have reiterated our recommendations that Management:
 Require the conduct of annual physical count of PPE in accordance with
Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Vol. 1;
 Invite a representative of the Auditor to witness the inventory taking for this
purpose;
 Require the maintenance of complete and accurate Equipment Ledger
Cards/ PPELC for each inventory item pursuant to Section 488 (d) of the
GAAM and the NGAS in order to promote effective monitoring and controls
over PPE;
 Require the reconciliation of the Property and Accounting records after the
physical count;
 Physical Inventory Reports shall be submitted to the Property Officer and
COA Office for verification and reconciliation of the result of the physical
inventory with the complete Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards
and the Property Cards maintained by the Accounting Office and Property
Unit, respectively;
 Require the renewal of Property Acknowledgment Receipt (PAR) every
January of the third year after issue in accordance with Section 492 of
GAAM Vol. 1; and
 Comply with the prescribed procedures in COA Circular No. 89-296 on
disposal of unserviceable PPE, if any.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Budget Officer maintain the four (4) registries as required in Section 4(h) of
the Manual on NGAS to monitor appropriations, allotments received and
obligations incurred against said allotment and in order to prevent
incurrence of obligations in excess of allotments received or approved
appropriations. Said registries shall also be the basis in preparing the
Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts.

We have reiterated our recommendations that Management exert more
effort to establish the existence and validity of the receivable accounts, and
furnish COA with a copy of any relevant record containing sufficient
information about the foregoing receivable balances in order to effect
collection thereof. If not, Management may file request for authority to
write-off dormant receivable accounts to COA following the procedures
stated in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

Also, the Municipal Accountant should exert more effort to trace the original
entry made and make the necessary adjustments on the dormant payable
accounts to present fairly the financial statements. Payables maintained in
the books of accounts shall be properly documented and supported with
valid claims of specific creditors, processed vouchers or payrolls on
withholdings, demand payments or bills. Likewise, the Accounting Unit
should maintain complete and updated subsidiary ledgers for Receivable and
Payable accounts.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare monthly
transmittal of submitted disbursement vouchers, official receipts, payrolls
and journal entry vouchers to the Municipal Accountant, who after
recording in the Books of Accounts, shall then submit the same to the
Auditor for the conduct of timely audit so as to facilitate timely
communication of noted deficiencies. This way, management could easily
pinpoint the persons responsible and determine the cause of delay of
submission of accounts and reports.

We have recommended that management justify the above-noted
findings. Henceforth, ensure that the amounts budgeted and
disbursed for PS are within the limit as set forth by Section 325 (a) of
R.A. No. 7160 and DBM Local Budget Circular No. 98. No additional
appropriation and payments should be made for PS items in excess of
the limit.

We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit
written justifications particularly on the above-noted conflicting dates in the
bidding and procurement process as well as the other deficiencies noted for
non-compliance with the provisions of R.A. No. 9184. Henceforth, BAC
ensure that the procuring entity strictly follow the standards set forth by the
procurement law and its Revised IRR.

We have recommended that management submit written explanations on the
noted deficiencies and enforce the collection of the liquidated damages to the
contractors. The MPDC/TWG and the MDRRMO/Acting Municipal
Engineer determine the percentage of completion as of the target completion
date for the said projects and compute for the amount of the liquidated
damages. Henceforth, the LGU should properly monitor all its projects and
in the event that the contractor fail to accomplish the project within the
expected delivery period/contract duration, the related liquidated damages
and other appropriate actions be enforced immediately.

In view of the foregoing, we have recommended that:
 The BAC members, BAC secretariat and TWG update and familiarize
themselves with different rules and regulations on procurements as set forth
by competent authority;
 Management use DPWH Department Order No. 72, series of 2016 or its
updated issuances on the matter in its succeeding computation of the ABC of
infrastructure projects for CY 2017 onwards; and
 Justify the above noted lapses.

We have recommended that Management require the concerned personnel to
submit the required supporting documents to COA for audit purposes.
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant see to it that succeeding transactions
be properly supported with complete documentation and in accordance with
pertinent laws, rules and regulations in order to establish the validity and
propriety of the project execution and transactions/disbursements.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the personnel in-
charge with release of contracts and bidding documents to furnish the
auditor with copies of these documents together with the required
attachments/supporting papers within five (5) days after perfection thereof
as required by COA Circular No. 2009-001 for auditorial and technical
review.

We have recommended that management, in cases of lump sum
appropriations containing general terms, seek a separate SB appropriation
ordinance/resolution to serve as an authority to enter into contract in behalf
of the Municipal Government in accordance to Section 22 (c) of R.A. No.
7160 and the applicable jurisprudence.

We have recommended that the municipal mayor, before entering into
contract in behalf of the Municipal Government, should ensure the existence
of an appropriation in accordance with Section 7 of R.A. No. 9184, Section
85(1) of P.D. No. 1445, Section 22 (c) of R.A. No. 7160 and the afore-cited
jurisprudence. Fund availability is, as it has always been, an indispensable
prerequisite to the execution of any government contract involving the
expenditure of public funds by all government agencies at all levels.

We have recommended that Management justify the above-noted lapses.
Utilization of the 20% Development Fund should be limited on programs,
projects and activities that will contribute to the attainment of socio-
economic development and environmental management outcomes and shall
partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures and yield greater
benefit to the people in accordance with DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2011-1.

We have recommended that management submit monthly Report on the
Receipt and Utilization of the DRRMF sourced from GAA and Report on the
Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations of the municipality to NDRRMC
through the OCD on or before the 5th day following the end of each month,
and furnish copy to the audit team for audit/evaluation.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget
Officer and the Municipal Mayor ensure that CDUR are attached to the DV
before the utilization of cash donation for DRRM for proper monitoring and
control. Also, the Accounting Unit shall prepare and maintain separate
subsidiary ledger (SL) for "Trust Liabilities-DRRMF" for cash donations
received amounting to ₱100,000.00 and above per donor per purpose while
those below ₱100,000.00 shall be grouped and posted in one SL labeled as
"Various Donors" per purpose.

We have recommended that management see to it that in cases of lump sum
appropriations containing general terms, a separate SB appropriation
ordinance/ resolution must always be secured by the Municipal Mayor to
serve as an authority to enter into contract in behalf of the Municipal
Government in accordance to Section 22(c) of R.A. No. 7160 and the
applicable jurisprudence.

We have recommended that management strictly comply with the provisions
of R.A. No. 10121 and the related subsequent provisions on LDRRMF
utilization. Personal services, maintenance and other operating
expenditures, and capital outlay of the LDRRMO as well as the regular
MOOE and capital outlay of the municipality shall be sourced from the
General Fund. The LDRRMF shall be strictly utilized for the
implementation of DRRM programs.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the list of
recipients or beneficiaries duly signed/acknowledged. The listings should
also include the quantity and quantity of items distributed for proper
documentation and for audit purposes.

We have recommended that management comply with the regulations on the
transfer of unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF to the Trust
Fund.
